title: Berlin Split: The Lost Archive
subtitle: Can you read Berlin before the Wall disappears?

acts.label: Act 1
acts.title: The City Looks Simple
acts.lead: The first files look obvious. Berlin rarely is.

acts.label: Act 2
acts.title: The Border Gets Complicated
acts.lead: The line on the map starts to behave less like a line.

acts.label: Act 3
acts.title: The City Rebuilds Itself
acts.lead: After 1990, Berlin does not simply erase the split. It rebuilds around it.

archivePieces.name: TV Tower Signal
archivePieces.description: A file for the East Berlin square that still dominates the skyline.

archivePieces.name: Red City Seal
archivePieces.description: Proof that some Berlin symbols are older than the division.

archivePieces.name: Island Fragment
archivePieces.description: Imperial, damaged, restored, and still being interpreted.

archivePieces.name: Transit Stamp
archivePieces.description: A crossing file for Friedrichstrasse and the divided railway city.

archivePieces.name: Kiez Echo
archivePieces.description: A West Berlin neighborhood shaped by the nearby border.

archivePieces.name: Painted Wall
archivePieces.description: A border remnant that became a memorial and an image machine.

archivePieces.name: Palast Shadow
archivePieces.description: A vanished GDR building beneath a rebuilt palace facade.

archivePieces.name: Void Map
archivePieces.description: A map piece from the empty zone that became a new center.

archivePieces.name: Park Strip
archivePieces.description: A former border strip that turned into a Sunday Berlin ritual.

archivePieces.name: Final Ledger
archivePieces.description: The file that proves Berlin is read in layers, not sides.

missions.title: Alexanderplatz Signal
missions.instruction: Stamp the archive layer this evidence belongs to.
missions.evidence: A wide socialist-era square, the TV Tower above it, and the World Time Clock pulling everyone toward the same meeting point.
missions.clue: The square has older roots, but its tourist image was powerfully shaped by East Berlin urban planning.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: The postwar image points strongly east.
options.feedback: The file locks. Alexanderplatz is older than the GDR, but the version tourists read first is unmistakably East Berlin.

options.label: West Berlin layer
options.detail: Treat it like a polished West Berlin center.
options.feedback: The archive rejects the polish. This is not the Ku'damm story; it is the East Berlin center tourists still photograph.

options.label: Border zone layer
options.detail: Read it as a crossing point.
options.feedback: A tempting stamp, but the square was not the crossing logic. Its power is central East Berlin identity.

options.label: Layered city layer
options.detail: Older city plus GDR image.
options.feedback: Good instinct. The archive accepts the nuance, but the mission file wants the East Berlin signal.

missions.title: Rotes Rathaus File
missions.instruction: Place the seal without letting the red brick fool you.
missions.evidence: A red-brick city hall near Alexanderplatz. It looks like a simple East Berlin clue, but its story reaches back before the Cold War.
missions.clue: The building became part of East Berlin administration, but it was not born as a GDR building.
missions.stereotype: Everything in the East-looking center is GDR architecture.

options.label: Layered city layer
options.detail: Older Berlin, later divided-city use.
options.feedback: The seal holds. Rotes Rathaus is exactly the kind of building Berlin uses to trick fast readers.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: Read it only through GDR administration.
options.feedback: The archive bends, not breaks. East Berlin used it, but the building is older than that file.

options.label: West Berlin layer
options.detail: Move it to the western civic record.
options.feedback: The seal smears. This was not the West Berlin city hall story.

options.label: Rebuilt city layer
options.detail: Treat it mainly as a post-1990 symbol.
options.feedback: Too late. Reunification changes the frame, but the file begins much earlier.

missions.title: Museum Island Damage Scan
missions.instruction: Sort the damage report into the right archive tray.
missions.evidence: Columns, bullet scars, royal collections, war damage, restoration choices, and tourists moving through the same stones.
missions.clue: This is not a clean East/West file. It is imperial Berlin, war, GDR-era restoration, and today at once.
missions.stereotype: Berlin history can be sorted into one period at a time.

options.label: Layered city layer
options.detail: Imperial, damaged, restored, and present-day.
options.feedback: The scan stabilizes. Museum Island is a layered archive, not a single regime label.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: Focus on its East Berlin location.
options.feedback: The file is incomplete. Location matters, but the island carries much older and wider traces.

options.label: West Berlin layer
options.detail: Move the museum prestige west.
options.feedback: The archive rejects it. Museum Island sits in the old center, not in the West Berlin prestige file.

options.label: Rebuilt city layer
options.detail: Treat the present restoration as the main story.
options.feedback: Close, but too modern. Restoration is one layer, not the whole island.

missions.title: Friedrichstrasse Checkpoint
missions.instruction: Choose the route that keeps the transit record intact.
missions.evidence: A station, a border procedure, a farewell hall, and a shopping street that still looks too normal for what happened here.
missions.clue: Friedrichstrasse was both everyday transit and emotional border theatre.
missions.stereotype: The Wall only mattered where you can still see concrete.

options.label: Border crossing record
options.detail: The border hides inside transport and paperwork.
options.feedback: The stamp lands. Friedrichstrasse proves the border was also a routine, a platform, and a goodbye.

options.label: Shopping-street reading
options.detail: Ignore the crossing and read the storefronts.
options.feedback: The file loses pressure. The street matters, but this mission is about the border operating inside normal city movement.

options.label: Visible Wall only
options.detail: Only trust visible Wall remains.
options.feedback: The archive shakes. Berlin's border story is not always a slab of concrete.

options.label: Modern street layer
options.detail: Treat it as a reunified shopping corridor.
options.feedback: Too clean. The modern street sits on top of a much heavier crossing file.

missions.title: Kreuzberg Border Echo
missions.instruction: Mark what the neighborhood was carrying.
missions.evidence: A West Berlin Kiez pressed close to the Wall: cheap rents, migrant life, political edges, and streets that did not feel like postcard West Germany.
missions.clue: West Berlin was not a normal rich western city. It was isolated and politically strange.
missions.stereotype: West Berlin was simply polished and comfortable.

options.label: Border zone layer
options.detail: A western neighborhood shaped by the edge.
options.feedback: The echo is restored. Kreuzberg helps break the easy West equals polished myth.

options.label: West Berlin layer
options.detail: File it as straightforward West Berlin.
options.feedback: The archive accepts the side but not the simplification. Kreuzberg was West Berlin with border pressure.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: Read alternative culture as an East clue.
options.feedback: The file jumps sides. Alternative Berlin did not map neatly onto East Berlin.

options.label: Rebuilt city layer
options.detail: Treat the Kiez as mostly post-1990 image.
options.feedback: Too late again. Today's image has older border-era roots.

missions.title: Painted Wall Memory
missions.instruction: Stabilize the Wall fragment without turning it into only a photo backdrop.
missions.evidence: Painted concrete, crowds, river light, souvenir poses, and a memorial trying to stay louder than the Instagram frame.
missions.clue: This is Wall remnant, open-air gallery, memorial, tourist icon, and post-1989 transformation at once.
missions.stereotype: The Wall is only a dramatic tourist backdrop now.

options.label: Layered city layer
options.detail: Memorial, art, border remnant, and tourist symbol.
options.feedback: The paint settles. The Wall survives here because the meaning keeps changing.

options.label: Border zone layer
options.detail: Keep only the Cold War border layer.
options.feedback: Strong, but narrow. The border layer is real; the gallery layer is now part of the evidence too.

options.label: Rebuilt city layer
options.detail: Treat it mainly as a post-1989 art site.
options.feedback: The archive wants more weight. The art exists because this was border concrete.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: File it only by its eastern-side location.
options.feedback: Side alone is too small. This fragment became a citywide memory object.

missions.title: Palast Shadow Alignment
missions.instruction: Slide the vanished building shadow until the archive recognizes the present facade.
missions.evidence: A palace facade, a missing GDR parliament and cultural building, and a rebuilt center that still argues with itself.
missions.clue: The archive stabilizes when the old Palast shadow sits mostly behind the new palace file, not fully erased and not fully dominant.
missions.stereotype: Reunified Berlin simply removed the East and moved on.
missions.successFeedback: The shadow aligns. This site is not just a new facade; it is a public argument about what Berlin chooses to rebuild.
missions.nearFeedback: The image flickers. You can feel the conflict, but the Palast shadow is not quite placed.
missions.failFeedback: The facade tears away from the file. The point is not to erase one building or crown another; it is to read the replacement.

missions.title: Potsdamer Platz Void
missions.instruction: Stamp the file that explains the missing center.
missions.evidence: A place that once stood busy, then became border emptiness, then returned as a corporate-new city center.
missions.clue: The strange part is the gap: destruction, border zone, empty land, then post-1990 rebuilding.
missions.stereotype: Modern Berlin grew naturally from old Berlin without interruption.

options.label: Rebuilt city layer
options.detail: A post-1990 center built over a void.
options.feedback: The void fills in. Potsdamer Platz makes the missing years visible if you know where to look.

options.label: West Berlin layer
options.detail: Treat it as a West Berlin business center.
options.feedback: The archive refuses the shortcut. The site touched the western side, but the key evidence is the border void and rebuild.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: Treat it as an East Berlin redevelopment.
options.feedback: The pressure is off. This file is about a former center emptied by the border and remade after reunification.

options.label: Border zone layer
options.detail: Keep it only in the Wall zone.
options.feedback: The border layer is real, but the mission needs the rebuild after the void.

missions.title: Mauerpark Strip
missions.instruction: Recover the Sunday park without losing the border strip beneath it.
missions.evidence: Flea market energy, karaoke crowds, grass, families, tourists, and a name that still says Wall.
missions.clue: The park works because a former edge became a shared social space.
missions.stereotype: If a place feels fun now, the old border story is gone.

options.label: Former border strip
options.detail: A former edge turned into public ritual.
options.feedback: The park recovers. Berlin often hides heavy history under very ordinary weekend life.

options.label: Present-day park layer
options.detail: Focus on the post-1990 park life.
options.feedback: Close. The present-day park matters, but the name is still telling you what was there.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: Read the Wall name as East Berlin only.
options.feedback: The file narrows too much. The border shaped both sides of the edge.

options.label: West Berlin layer
options.detail: Read the park as a western cultural scene.
options.feedback: The archive tilts. Today's culture is real, but the old border strip is the key.

missions.title: Final Delivery
missions.instruction: Seal the archive with the reading Berlin needs.
missions.evidence: A final walk from the old center into today's city: courtyards, trains, nightlife, restored facades, and memory under the surface.
missions.clue: The archive is complete only when Berlin is read as a layered city, not as a scoreboard between sides.
missions.stereotype: Berlin can be explained by picking one side.

options.label: Layered city layer
options.detail: Berlin is read through overlapping times, borders, and rebuilds.
options.feedback: The archive closes cleanly. You did not just cross Berlin; you learned how to read it.

options.label: East Berlin layer
options.detail: Finish with the old center as an East Berlin story.
options.feedback: The archive stays open. The East layer is real, but it is not the whole ending.

options.label: West Berlin layer
options.detail: Finish with today's consumer city.
options.feedback: The file loses its spine. Modern life is visible, but it sits on older lines.

options.label: Rebuilt city layer
options.detail: Finish with reunification and restoration.
options.feedback: Almost. Rebuild is a major layer, but Berlin keeps more than one file open.

endings.title: Lost Tourist
endings.description: The archive collapsed, but Berlin did what Berlin does: it made the simple version impossible.
endings.tip: Retry from your last safe file and read the evidence before the side.

endings.title: Checkpoint Rookie
endings.description: You crossed the story, but the archive is full of warning stamps.
endings.tip: A cleaner run means fewer clues and fewer rushed stamps.

endings.title: Border Reader
endings.description: You can spot the obvious line and some of the hidden ones.
endings.tip: The next level is reading places that look ordinary now.

endings.title: Wall Detective
endings.description: You read the split without turning Berlin into a cartoon of East and West.
endings.tip: Try a run with fewer clues to protect more of the archive.

endings.title: Archive Keeper
endings.description: The files survived, the map makes sense, and the city stayed complicated.
endings.tip: A no-clue clean run can still unlock the top rank.

endings.title: Myth Breaker
endings.description: You broke the tourist version of Berlin and kept the archive almost intact.
endings.tip: This is the run worth sharing.
